notes: Epic Stack app, not a published package. No component dist — synth/no-dist mode: `.design-sync/entry.tsx` is a barrel re-exporting the curated 9 components (kept in lockstep with app/components/styleguide/specimens.tsx; enforced by app/components/styleguide/design-sync.test.ts). DropdownMenu/Tooltip/InputOTP are compound — the barrel also exports their sub-parts (DropdownMenuContent, TooltipTrigger, InputOTPSlot, …) so the design agent can compose them; the lockstep test maps each sub-part back to its canonical root by name prefix. --entry points esbuild at that barrel and makes PKG_DIR resolve to the repo root (node_modules/epic-stack-template does not exist; the app isn't self-installed). cssEntry is the Tailwind v4 COMPILED stylesheet — the raw app/styles/tailwind.css is only @import 'tailwindcss' + token vars and has no utility classes. On re-sync: run `pnpm build`, then `pnpm design-sync:css` (globs the hashed build/client/assets/tailwind-*.css → .design-sync/styles.compiled.css; the hash in the filename changes each build, so do not hand-copy). #app/* imports resolve via package.json imports; @/icon-name resolves via tsconfig.json paths (tsconfigPathsPlugin); icons/sprite.svg is inlined via the .svg dataurl loader. Card groups (Actions/Forms/etc. from manifest.json) are applied at register_assets time, not derived by the converter. guidelinesGlob is [] on purpose: the default glob sweeps docs/*.md, which here are Epic Stack operational docs (database/deployment/caching/...), not design guidance. [CSS_RUNTIME] is expected and non-blocking — the Tailwind token vars + utilities all live in _ds_bundle.css (linked alongside styles.css per the README contract), so tokens/ being empty is cosmetic; components still render styled. All 9 component previews under .design-sync/previews/ are HAND-OWNED (no @ds-preview marker line) — they mirror the variant grids in specimens.tsx (Button variants+sizes, StatusButton 4 states, Input 3 states, Checkbox 3 states, Label+Input field, Textarea, plus the DropdownMenu/Tooltip/InputOTP compound specimens rendered open). The previews dir is held one-to-one with the curated set by design-sync.test.ts. The converter leaves marker-less files untouched, so previewArgs/dtsPropsFor are the only props config that matters; do not re-add previewArgs for these 9. NOT curated: icon.tsx (a sprite primitive — see the KNOWN LIMITATION below on <use> glyphs not painting) and sonner.tsx (an imperative toaster with no static specimen). dtsPropsFor hand-writes each <Name>Props because synth/no-dist mode has no shipped component .d.ts and findTypesRoot resolves the repo's types/ dir (icon-name only), so auto-extraction degrades to an index signature. KNOWN LIMITATION: StatusButton's status glyphs (pending spinner / success check / error cross) do not paint in the bundle/previews. icon.tsx does `<use href={`${spriteDataUrl}#name`}>` and esbuild inlines icons/sprite.svg as a data: URL; Chrome refuses to resolve <use> fragment refs into a data-URL document. The real app works because it serves sprite.svg as a same-origin file. The button + states + text render correctly; only the inner glyph is missing. Proper fix needs the sprite served as a same-origin asset or its <symbol>s inlined into the host document (a claude.ai/design environment concern) — not fixable via converter config without forking bundle.mjs (discouraged).
